tldr: Move from Cloudinary to Bytescale, a similar upload-and-transform API with simpler subscription pricing and a smaller surface area.
why_switch: Comparable upload + on-the-fly transformation feature set for image use cases
Lighter platform may be a better fit for teams not using Cloudinary's advanced features
Lower lock-in profile — uploaded files remain accessible as standard URLs
Faster to onboard for small/medium media workloads
what_to_export: All original assets from the Cloudinary Media Library
Tags and metadata attached to assets
Active transformation recipes used in production URLs
List of asset URLs currently embedded in apps, CMS, emails
Any derived assets you cannot regenerate
export_method: Use Cloudinary's Admin API or CLI to list and download originals in batches, capturing public_id, folder, tags, and metadata into a manifest. For very large libraries, contact Cloudinary support for bulk export options. Refer to Cloudinary's official documentation for current export procedures.
import_method: Create a Bytescale account, generate API keys, and use Bytescale's official upload API/SDK to push assets while preserving your folder layout. Re-implement your transformation recipes using Bytescale's URL parameters per their docs. Update application code and content references to point to the new Bytescale URLs.

concept_mapping.note: Different syntax — rewrite per Bytescale docs
Use Bytescale signing per their docs
Verify exact parameter names in current docs

gotchas: Transformation URL syntax differs — every transformed URL in your codebase must be rewritten
Verify advanced Cloudinary features (AI tagging, video manipulation) have equivalents before committing
Smaller vendor footprint means fewer integrations and SDKs — check yours is supported
Plan for a dual-write or redirect period to avoid broken images during cutover
Confirm bandwidth and storage limits on the Bytescale plan you choose
Test responsive image breakpoints carefully after migration

email.subject: Cancelling Cloudinary subscription — moving to Bytescale
email.body:
Hi Cloudinary team,

We're switching our media handling to Bytescale and would like to wind down our Cloudinary subscription at the end of the current billing cycle. Please confirm the cancellation procedure and any data retention timeline once the account is closed.

Also, could you confirm the recommended approach for a full bulk export of our originals?

Thanks for the service.

Best regards
